claudeMd:
# Task: Invert Left-Right Ball Movement

You are fixing the lateral movement direction in a browser-based head-tilt ball game (Teeter).

## Codebase

Pure static HTML+JS served by nginx. No npm, no build step.
- `index.html` — Main page with Three.js importmap and overlay UI
- `js/main.js` — Game loop, state management
- `js/tracker.js` — MediaPipe FaceLandmarker head tilt detection (DO NOT MODIFY)
- `js/physics.js` — Ball physics (lateral movement, forward speed, falling)
- `js/renderer.js` — Three.js scene (track, ball, camera, lighting)
- `Dockerfile` + `nginx.conf` — Docker build serving on port 8080

## What to Fix

The ball currently moves in the opposite lateral direction from the user's head tilt. Tilting right moves the ball left, and vice versa.

### Root Cause

In `js/physics.js` line 49, the tilt angle is mapped directly to lateral velocity without inverting:
```js
const targetVx = tiltAngle * DIRECT_SENSITIVITY;
```

The sign needs to be flipped.

### Fix

Change line 49 of `js/physics.js` from:
```js
const targetVx = tiltAngle * DIRECT_SENSITIVITY;
```
to:
```js
const targetVx = -tiltAngle * DIRECT_SENSITIVITY;
```

This is the ONLY change needed. Do not modify any other file.

## Conventions
- No npm/node — pure static JS with ES module imports
- Code style: 2-space indent, single quotes, const/let (no var)
- DO NOT modify `js/tracker.js`
- Keep changes minimal — this is a one-line fix

## Testing
- `docker build -t teeter .` must succeed
- Visual verification: when tilting head right, ball should move right; when tilting head left, ball should move left

checks: The ball moves to the RIGHT side of the track when the user tilts their head to the RIGHT
The ball moves to the LEFT side of the track when the user tilts their head to the LEFT
The ball still responds smoothly to head tilt changes without jitter or delay
All other gameplay remains functional: forward movement, falling off edges, obstacle collisions, coin collection, and score tracking
